title: Oceanside Pavement Condition Projections — Calibrated Model
methodology: Bin-based PCI degradation model with 1-point resolution. Calibrated against city's FY20-21 actual treatment data: 12 mi overlay ($4.6M, +35 PCI pts) + 46 mi slurry ($992K, +8 PCI pts). Historical spending 2014-2025 from existing model estimates, then scenario spending 2026-2035. Overlay applied worst-first, slurry applied preventively to fair/good roads.

caveats: PCI data from July 2014 — 12 years old. Model starts there and simulates forward.
Historical spending estimates (FY14-25) are approximations, not verified from ACFRs.
Model assumes worst-first overlay strategy, which cities don't always follow.
No inflation adjustment — all costs in constant 2022 dollars.
Degradation rates are estimates — actual rates vary by base material, traffic, climate.
Model shows bimodal distribution (patchwork effect) which is realistic for underfunded programs.

spending_gap_summary.pct_of_need_funded: 36-49% of maintenance need, 20% of repair need
